author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
content:
MEDICINE — DAILY PROGRESS NOTE
Hospital day 5 (pre-discharge planning).

SUBJECTIVE: Feels well. Minimal residual chest discomfort. Eager to return home and to work; concerned about the liver finding and possibility of cancer.

OBJECTIVE:
- Stable, off oxygen, ambulating without limitation. On therapeutic heparin infusion; no bleeding.
- Triple-phase liver CT (IMG005): non-diagnostic but concerning for malignancy; biopsy remains the recommended next step.
- Renal function normal; LFTs stable.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN (intentionally brief):
#Acute VTE — clinically resolved symptoms on anticoagulation. Plan to TRANSITION from heparin to apixaban for discharge.
#Liver mass — biopsy recommended; will be arranged as an OUTPATIENT in coordination with interventional radiology and hepatology.
#Discharge tomorrow if stable, with clear return precautions for recurrent VTE symptoms.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Daniel Okafor, MD (PGY-2) and Dr. Marisol Vega, MD. 7/10/2027.
